task_completion.description: Open the multi-layer image in the image editor.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The GIMP application window is open, displaying an image with a blue gradient background, a red circle, and yellow text.

task_completion.description: Ensure the Layers panel is visible to interact with the layers.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Layers dialog/dock is visible on the screen, showing the initial 3 layers (Text, Circle, Background).

task_completion.description: Select the top layer in the layer stack.
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the Layers panel, the top-most layer (the 'Text' layer) is highlighted or selected.
A context menu (right-click on the layer) or the top 'Layer' menu is open, and the 'Merge Down' option is being clicked or highlighted.

task_completion.description: Verify the top layer has been merged with the layer immediately below it.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Layers panel now shows exactly 2 layers instead of 3. The new top layer's thumbnail shows a combination of both the yellow text and the red circle.

integrity.description: The agent used the graphical user interface to merge the layers, not terminal commands.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show interaction with GIMP's menus and panels, without terminal windows running ImageMagick or GIMP batch scripts to bypass the GUI.

integrity.description: The agent interacted with the actual GIMP application.
integrity.visual_evidence: The standard GIMP interface (tools, docks, menus) is clearly visible and being used.

integrity.description: The visual appearance of the image on the canvas remains unchanged after the merge.
integrity.visual_evidence: The main image canvas still displays the yellow text over the red circle on the blue gradient background without any visual corruption or missing elements.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The task uses a 400x300px GIMP XCF file. Initially, there are exactly 3 layers: 'Text' (yellow text, top), 'Circle' (red circle, middle), and 'Background' (blue gradient, bottom). A successful 'Merge Down' operation from the top layer will result in exactly 2 layers remaining in the Layers panel, with the new top layer combining the text and the circle. The overall visual appearance on the main canvas must remain identical (yellow text over red circle on blue background).
